name: Atkinson, E. L.
motto: "Of science and logic he chatters, as fine and as fast as he can."

name: Baldwin, Kathleen
motto: "Consistent, wearing all that weight of learning lightly like a flower."
clubs: President of Y. W. C. A. 1914-'15

name: Bankston, Herman Jesse
motto: "A lion among ladies is a most dreadful thing."

name: Bell, William Dale Jr.
motto: "For thy sake, tobacco, I would do anything but die."

name: Bene, Wildie Dudley
motto: "A heart as soft, a heart as kind, A heart as large and free As in the whole world thou canst find."

name: Bethea, S. E.
motto: "Write me as one who loves his fellow-man."

name: Bradley, Howard Eugene
motto: "Who mix'd reason with pleasure, and wisdom with mirth, If he had any faults he has left us in doubt."

name: Brashears, Wiley W., Jr.
motto: "I do not ask, O Lord, that life may be a pleasant road."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Glee Club

name: Bridgforth, R. M.
motto: "He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one. Exceeding wise, fair spoken and persuading."

name: Buntin, Robert R.
motto: "As headstrong as an allegory on the bank of the Nile."

name: Carnes, Edwin Hammond
motto: "Besides 'tis known he could speak Greek. As naturally as pigs squeak; That Latin was no more difficult Than to a blackbird 'tis to whistle."

name: Caldwell, James H., Jr.
motto: "A town that boasts inhabitants like me, can have no lack of society."

name: Lemmie, R. Cleveland
motto: "My Liberty, was Dukedom large enough."

name: Marie, Byrd Dees
motto: "While bright-eyed science watches round."
major: B.S., B.A. from I. I. & C. '16
clubs: Chairman of Social Service Committee Y. W. C. A.

name: Perry, Eugene Duncan
motto: "Although he had much wit, He was very shy of using it."

name: Helen, Christine Farley
motto: "She has a glowing heart; they say, Tho calm her seeming be."

name: Fulton, A. Furr
motto: "Take-it-easy and live-long are brothers."

name: Furr, W. P.
motto: "And what is thy history? A blank, My Lord."

name: Gathings, J. C.
motto: "Men of few words are the best men."

name: Grant, Roy Gilmer
motto: "Let us have peace."; "Nature hath framed strange fellows in her time."

name: Brooks, D. Good
motto: "Night after night he sat, And bleared his eyes with books."

name: Jefferson, K. Hamm
motto: "Is this that haughty gallant, gay Lothario?"

name: Richard, Stother Harris
motto: "God bless the man who first invented sleep, So Sancho Panza said, and so say I."

name: Hedleston, Emily
motto: "A beautiful and a happy girl, With step as light as summer air."

name: Hiler, Mabel C.
motto: "And oftentimes arranged with care, A hundred times a single air."

name: Holloday, Walter R.
motto: "A man to be depended on."; "The time I've lost in wooing, In watching and pursuing The light that lies in woman's eyes, Has been my heart's undoing."

name: Johnson, Bernice
motto: "To be slow in words is woman's only virtue."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Y. W. C. A. '15
President of Y. W. C. A. '16

name: Kendel, Octavia Elizabeth
motto: "Tho' on pleasure she was bent, she had a frugal mind."

name: Kincannon, Leroy Taylor
motto: "We know that with the ladies He was always raising Hades."

name: Knight, Minnie Estelle
motto: "Serene and resolute and still And calm and self-possessed."

name: LaRue, Regnold Benjamin
motto: "With just enough learning to misquote."

name: Druie, Anselm McCall
motto: "Much may be made of a Scotchman, if he be caught young."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Council and Cabinet

name: James, McClure Jr.
motto: "He will lie, sir, with such volubility, that you would think Truth were a fool."; "Though I am splenetic and rash, Yet I have something in me dangerous."

name: Margaret, Moss
motto: "There was a soft and pensive grace, A cast of thought upon her face, That suited well her forehead."

name: Eunice, Noblin
motto: "Here Love his golden shafts employs, here lights His constant lamp, and waves his purple wings, Reigns here and revels."
clubs: Chairman Student Government Committee of Ricks Hall '16-'17

name: Lowe, James O.
motto: "One ear it heard, the other out it went."

name: Mabus, L. E.
motto: "Whose little body lodged a mighty mind."

name: Mayers, Alonzo Gustavus
motto: "A blameless body and a faultless mind."

name: Mccain, J. P.
motto: "Fie! What a spendthrift he is with his tongue."
clubs: Vice-President Philomathean Society and Magazine Staff, Mississippi College '13-'14
Alternate to Chautauqua Oratorical Contest and Inter-society Debating Team '15-'16
President and Anniversarian of Phi Sigma, and Y. M. C. A. Council '16-'17

name: Lampton, George Ben
motto: "I am not a politician, and my other habits are good."

name: Lawrence, George Malcolm
motto: "Let the world slide, let the world go; A fig for care, and a fig for woe. If I can't pay, why I can owe, And death makes equal the high and low."

name: Laws, John Alston
motto: "Oh, wise and most learned doctor!"

name: Linder, Esther
motto: "And keeps good humor still whate'er we lose."

name: Parkes, Olyn F.
motto: "Talks as familiarly of roaring lions As maids of thirteen do of puppy dogs."; "I am Sir Oracle, and when I open my lips let no dog bark."

name: Parks, Margie
motto: "Her glossy hair was clustered over a brow Bright with intelligence and fair and smooth."

name: Perry, Wendel H.
motto: "And since I say it, Why, Marry Look you, it must be true."; "I'm the king at the balls, I'm a prince in banquet halls."

name: Rainwater, Percy Lee
motto: "Fired that the house rejects him, So death I'll print it, And shame the fools."
clubs: Phi Sigma Representative in Intersociety Debate '16
Ole Miss Representative in Ole Miss-Millsaps Debate

name: Ross, James Allen
motto: "Let ignorance talk as it will, learning has its value."
clubs: Secretary Philomathean Society '14-'15 at Mississippi College
President of the University Teachers' Club '16-'17

name: Sanderson, Miss Stuart
motto: "She'd prove a buzzard is no fool, And that a lord may be an owl, A calf an alderman, a goose a justice."

name: Sharman, Jackson R.
motto: "He was like the cock who thought The sun had risen to hear him crow."

name: Smith, Myrtle
motto: "Sober, steadfast, and demure."

name: Smith, Robert W.
motto: "A man after his own heart, but another's hand."; "No storm ever ruffled the current of his life."

name: Sumrall, Robbie
motto: "Knowledge and truth and virtue were her theme."

name: Wilson, Crate Le Master
motto: "But still his tongue ran on, the less of weight it bore, with greater ease."

name: Wilson, Malcolm Earl
motto: "Another good farmer spoiled."

name: Wilson, Willie Fayne
motto: "Hang sorrow! Care will kill a cat. And therefore let us be merry."

name: Young, Fred W.
motto: "Whence is thy learning? Doth thy toil over numerous books consume the midnight oil?"
clubs: Member of Board of Control of Mississippian '16-'17

name: Hopkins, George P.
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man."; "An honest man's the noblest work of God."

name: Guyton, Pearl
motto: "Inquiring, tireless, seeking what is yet unfound."

name: Marshall, Wilson Amis
motto: "And still they gazed and still their wonder grew. How one small head could carry all he knew."
clubs: Assistant Treasurer U. M. A. A. '14-'15

name: Bramlette, James Edwin Jr.
motto: "I may justly say with that hook-nosed fellow of Rome: I came, I saw, I overcame."

name: Barnett, Thomas Jefferson
motto: "I am no orator as Brutus is; I only speak right on."

name: Bridgforth, Robert Moore
motto: "A reading machine, always wound up and going. He mastered whatever was not worth the knowing."

name: Brooks, Rodney Lawrence
motto: "I am sure care is an enemy to life."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Junior Law Class '16
Lord High Constable of University Court '16-'17
Exalted Grand Drake of the Loyal Order of Ducks

name: Buntin, Robert Robson
motto: "A wit with dunces, and a dunce with wits."

name: Busby, Richard C.
motto: "If sandwiches are not plentiful where he came from it is not from want of tongue."
clubs: Interstate Intercollegiate Debater against University of Arkansas '16-'17

name: Campbell, Robert Matthew
motto: "Oh wad some power the giftie gie us, To see oursel's as others see us."

name: Clayton, William Luther
motto: "The strongest minds are those of whom the noisy world hears least."

name: Crook, Robert Hall
motto: "The noblest study of mankind is woman."

name: Dunning, Fred Stewart
motto: "A fellow of infinite jest, of most excellent fancy."
clubs: Honorary Member of the Loyal Order of Ducks

name: Friday, James Elmer
motto: "He mistook his calling."

name: Gilbert, James Jackson Glen
motto: "Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall never be disappointed."

name: Hairston, William Gray
motto: "He knew the precise psychological moment when to say nothing."
clubs: Assistant Business Manager of Annual "Woop-la" Club

name: Katzenmeyer, Charles Leonard A.
motto: "No question is ever settled until it is settled right."

name: Maples, Luther Whit
motto: "He trudged along, unknowing what he sought! And whistled as he went for want of thought."

name: Mitchell, Thomas Herron
motto: "Tho' modest on his unembarrassed brow, Nature has written, 'Gentleman'."
clubs: Secretary Hermaean Literary Society, third term '15

name: Murphy, Charles McBeth
motto: "Whose power shed around him a constant influence."

name: McCollough, William Lee
motto: "A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays, And confident tomorrows."

name: Nason, Richard Henry
motto: "Doubt truth to be a liar, But never doubt I love."
clubs: Vice-President of Phi Sigma Literary Society '15-'16
President of Phi Sigma Literary Society (second term) '16
Secretary and Treasurer of Co-operative Boarding Club '14-'15
Vice-President of the Junior Law Class '16

name: Newsom, Bruce Davenport
motto: "Not to know me, argues yourself unknown; The lowest of your throng."

name: O'Quin, Thomas Jefferson
motto: "And he is oft the wisest man, Who is not wise at all!"
clubs: First Orator of Phi Sigma Anniversary '15-'16

name: Palmer, James Ethel
motto: "His only crime— That most resplendent hair."

name: Ellis, William Patrick
motto: "What! is the jay more precious than the lark because his feathers are more beautiful?"
clubs: President of University Law Club (second term) '16-'17

name: Hiram, Jerome Patterson
motto: "Wisely and slow; they stumble that run fast."

name: Allen, Ripley Peebles
motto: "He doth indeed show some sparks that are like wit."

name: James, H. Price, Jr.
motto: "Lofty and sour to them that loved him not, But to those that sought him, sweet as summer."
clubs: Member Council Y. M. C. A.

name: Carey Lee, Ratcliff
motto: "Diffused knowledge immortalizes itself."

name: Robert Hoffman, Shackleford
motto: "This book is all my fault."

name: Adam, Smith
motto: "How much more a dunce that's been sent to roam, Excels a dunce that's been kept at home."

name: Other Forrest, Smith
motto: "I always get the better when I argue alone."
clubs: President of Phi Sigma Society (second term) '16-'17
Manager of Students' Co-operative Boarding Club '15-'16
Member of Board of Control of Mississippian '16-'17

name: Sweat, Noah Spurgeon
motto: "A noble boy, a brave, free-hearted, careless one."
clubs: Honorary Member of The Loyal Order of Ducks

name: Tubb, Talmage Brice
motto: "There's something in a flying horse, There's something in a huge balloon."

name: Williamson, Nate S.
motto: "There are two sides to every question; mine and the wrong side."
clubs: Interstate Intercollegiate Debater against University of Arkansas '16-'17

name: Wing, Albert Gresham
motto: "I awoke one morning and found myself famous."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Y. M. C. A.
Board of Control Gordon Hall Boarding Club '15-'16

name: Kennedy, Leroy Holland Jr.
motto: "He's a pleasing elf enough, but lazy as the devil."

name: Kennedy, William David
motto: "If naebody care for me, I'll care for naebody."

name: McWhorter, Sickey Nelson
motto: "The heroes are not all six feet tall; Large souls may dwell in bodies small."

name: Thompson, Charles Ernest
motto: "I would rather excel others in knowledge than in power."

name: Clarke, M. Cleveland
motto: "Yond Cassius has a lean and hungry look, He thinks too much; such men are dangerous."
clubs: Member of "Knights of the Empty Dome."

name: Chaille, C. Bernard
motto: "For he by geometric scale, Can take the size of pots of ale."
clubs: Member of "The Knights of the Empty Dome."

name: Oscar, B. Ray
motto: "If you want to run a line to Heaven or sink a shaft to hell."
clubs: Member of "Knights of the Empty Dome."

name: Claude, Woodfin
motto: "All his faults are such that one Loves him still the better for them."

name: Irwin, W. Barrett
motto: "And some that smile have in their hearts, I fear, millions of mischief."

name: J. A. K., Birchett
motto: "The cutest little boy that ever crowed for kisses."

name: Stanford, E. Bethea
motto: "Toiling, rejoicing, sorrowing, Onward through life he goes."

name: Jorge, C. Castellanos
motto: "The noblest mind the best contentment has."

name: Crawford, W. S.
motto: "Let all things be done decently and in order."

name: Curry, James F.
motto: "Takes a paradoxical view of whatever subject he approaches."

name: Funderburk, Joe M.
motto: "Virtue is like a rich stone—best when plain set."

name: Glass, Sydney H.
motto: "Least, but not least heard."

name: Brooks, David Good
motto: "He followeth knowledge like a setting star."

name: Charles, H. Kirkpatrick
motto: "Ripe in wisdom was he, but patient, and simple and childlike."

name: Lustberg, Sam R.
motto: "Thou art a fellow of good respect."

name: Mason, Herman E.
motto: "A moment lost can never be regained."

name: McLeod, Hugh Carroll
motto: "Warp your face if it be sour. I can spare a smile from mine."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Red and Blue Club

name: Neely, Paul Talmage
motto: "And to that dauntless temper of his mind He hath a wisdom that doth guide his valor To act in safety."

name: Pigott, Albert W.
motto: "Untwisting all the chains that tie The hidden soul of harmony."

name: Gonzalez, José Polanco
motto: "It is said the heart and not the brain That to the highest doth attain."

name: Schreier, Phil C.
motto: "Labor with what zeal we will, Something still remains undone!"

name: Taylor, Archie
motto: "Why should a man, whose blood is warm within, Sit like his grandsire, cut in alabaster?"

name: Tillson, William Derrick
motto: "Tread softly 'round this sacred heap, It guards ambition's restless sleep."

name: Wilson, Donald Y.
motto: "There is always work, and tools to work withal, for those who will."

name: Holoway, P. D.
motto: "We grant although he has much wit, He is very fond of showing it."

name: Webb, W. C.
motto: "He thinks too little and talks too much."

name: Davis, Russell B.
motto: "Rises early, studies late, Works all day with book and slate."

name: Avent, James Kirk
motto: "I do remember an apothecary And hereabouts he dwells."

name: Azlin, Arthur Paul
motto: "I will wear my heart on my sleeve For the daws to peck at."

name: Blake, Benjamin Clifton
motto: "A man that fortune's buffets and rewards Hast ta'en with equal thanks."

name: Furr, William Prentiss
motto: "I am not merry but I do beguile The thing I am by seeming otherwise."

name: Gathright, Obadiah Holder
motto: "Since knowledge is but sorrow's spy, 'Tis not safe to know."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Pharmacy Class

name: Geisenberger, A. H.
motto: "Few hearts like this, with virtue warm'd, Few heads with knowledge so informed."

name: Hoke, D. W.
motto: "The man in life wherever plac'd Hath happiness in store."

name: Pigott, C. D.
motto: "But come what will, I've sworn it still, I'll ne'er be melancholy."

name: Pigott, J. E.
motto: "The man of independent mind, He looks and laughs at a' that."

name: Rowan, L. F.
motto: "Happy am I, from care I'm free, Why aren't they all contented like me?"

name: Tirey, V. J.
motto: "Here's metal more attractive."

name: Wheeler, Ralph E.
motto: "What wind blew you hither?"

name: Whittington, C. E.
motto: "Some called it brilliance, And others tapped their heads."
